basic_details.job_title: HNGU Teaching Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 17 Assistant Professor Posts
basic_details.short_information: HNGU , Patan is recruiting 17 Assistant Professor posts across various departments, last date for online application 15 June 2026.

important_dates.shortlisting / screening test: To be notified on University website
important_dates.interview_date: To be notified on University website

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the application processing fee through online mode only. Fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

qualification.education_qualification: Master's degree with minimum 55% marks (without grace marks) in the concerned/relevant/allied subject from an Indian University, or equivalent from an accredited foreign university + NET/SLET/SET cleared OR Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations (2009/2016)
qualification.required_degree: Ph.D. from a foreign university ranked in Top 500 in QS/THE/ARWU rankings also qualifies
qualification.details: As per UGC Regulations on Minimum Qualifications for Appointment of Teachers and Other Academic Staff in Universities and Colleges and Measures for the Maintenance of Standards in Higher Education 2018, and its amendments from time to time: | Option A: | Master's degree with 55% marks (without grace marks or rounding off), in a concerned/relevant/allied subject from an Indian University, or an equivalent degree from an accredited foreign university. PLUS: NET conducted by UGC/CSIR, or SLET/SET, or Ph.D. awarded as per UGC Minimum Standards Regulations (2009 or 2016). Candidates registered for Ph.D. prior to July 11, 2009 are exempt from NET/SLET/SET subject to fulfillment of conditions: Ph.D. awarded in regular mode, thesis evaluated by at least two external examiners, open viva voce conducted, two research papers published (at least one in a refereed journal), and at least two papers presented at UGC/ICSSR/CSIR-funded conferences. | Option B: | Ph.D. degree from a foreign university/institution ranked among the Top 500 in World University Rankings by QS, THE, or ARWU (Shanghai Jiao Tong University) at any time. | Marks Relaxation: | 5% relaxation (55% to 50%) at Master's level for SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/Differently-abled candidates. 5% relaxation (55% to 50%) for Ph.D. holders who obtained their Master's degree prior to 19th September 1991. | As per Minimum Standards of Architectural Education Regulations, 2020 and amendments: | B.Arch. with minimum 60% marks and 3 years of relevant professional experience; OR B.Arch. + Master's degree in Architecture or allied subjects with minimum 60% marks at either level and 1 year of relevant professional experience.

selection_process: Selection for the post of Assistant Professor will be based on:
Stage 1 - Academic Score Shortlisting: Candidates will be shortlisted based on Academic Score as per Appendix II
subject to fulfilling all essential eligibility criteria. Shortlisting ratio is 1:15 (suitable candidates with higher merit); if eligible candidates are fewer than 1:15

Stage 2 - Screening Test (wherever necessary): The University may hold a Screening Test for the post of Assistant Professor wherever necessary. Syllabus and schedule of such test will be published on the University website.

Stage 3 - Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for interview by email only. Candidates must appear at their own cost; no TA/DA will be paid. Call letters for interview will be sent only to short-listed candidates by email.
Table 3A) is used only for short-listing purposes. Final selection will be based solely on performance in the interview.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online through the University's recruitment portal at https://recruitment.HNGU .ac.in. Additionally, a physical hardcopy of the filled application form along with all supporting documents must be submitted separately.
Step-by-Step Process:
Visit https://recruitment.HNGU .ac.in and register/login.
Fill in the online application form completely and accurately.
Pay the application fee online before the last date.
Submit the online application on or before 15-06-2026, 05:00 PM.
Take a printout of the filled online application form - 3 sets (1 Original + 2 Photocopies).
Attach all self-attested testimonials: Educational Qualification Certificates, Experience Certificate, NOC (if applicable), Payment Receipt, and all supporting documents.
Also submit 3 copies of Academic Score (Appendix II, Table 3A of UGC Regulation 2018) in the prescribed proforma (available on https://recruitment.HNGU .ac.in).
Send the sealed cover through Speed Post or deliver in person to:
On the envelope, superscribe: "Post Applied for: [Name of Post] of [Name of Department]"
Physical submission must reach by 20-06-2026, 06:00 PM.
Documents Required (to attach with Physical Application):
Self-attested copies of all certificates of Age, Educational Qualifications (graduation onwards, with marksheets), Caste/Category, Physical Disability, Experience, NOC, etc.
Academic Score Appendix II Table 3A with supporting evidence documents in the same order as claims made.
Self-attested copy of peer-reviewed Journal / UGC-listed Journal proof for research papers published.
Government-issued photo identity proof (self-attested photocopy).
If name has changed: Marriage Certificate / Gazette notification / Aadhaar Card.
Candidates with foreign degrees: Equivalence Certificate from AIU.
Apply Online at: https://recruitment.HNGU .ac.in For Queries: est@HNGU .ac.in | Phone: 02766–237000
important_instructions: Online application must be filled at https://recruitment.HNGU .ac.in; no other mode is accepted. Physical submission of 03 (Three) hardcopy sets [01 Original + 02 Photocopies] along with all documents must reach the Registrar on or before 20-06-2026, 06:00 PM through Speed Post or in person. Candidates applying for more than one post must submit separate online applications and separate hardcopies with separate fees for each post. The University reserves the right to withdraw the advertisement partly/wholly, fill or not fill posts, or increase/decrease the number of posts at any time without giving any reason. Mere eligibility does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview; shortlisting is strictly based on Academic Score. Any additional qualifications, experience, or papers published after the last date of application will not be taken into consideration for the interview. Canvassing in any form on behalf of or by any candidate will result in disqualification. The age of retirement/superannuation will be as per Government of Gujarat norms/UGC/Council norms from time to time. In case of any dispute, the decision of the Vice-Chancellor/Board of Management, HNGU , Patan shall be final. Legal jurisdiction shall be Patan only. All changes/amendments/updates will be hosted only on the University website www.HNGU .ac.in; candidates must check regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in NGU Recruitment 2026 Advertisement No.05/2026?
faq.answer: Hemchandracharya North Gujarat University, Patan has announced a total of 17 Assistant Professor posts across 8 departments/institutes under Advertisement No. 05/2026 dated 22-05-2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for NGU Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application submission is 15-06-2026 up to 05:00 PM. The last date for physical hardcopy submission is 20-06-2026 up to 06:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary / pay scale for NGU Assistant Professor 2026? A. Selected
faq.answer: candidates will be placed at Level-10 with a basic pay of Rs. 57,700/- per month, as per State Government/UGC norms.

faq.question: Q4. Is NET/SLET/SET mandatory for NGU Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: NET/SLET/SET is the minimum eligibility for Assistant Professor posts (Arts, Science, Commerce & Management). However, candidates who hold a Ph.D. degree as per UGC Regulations 2009 or 2016 are exempted from the NET/SLET/SET requirement. For Architecture posts, NET/SLET/SET is not required; professional experience and B.Arch. marks are the criteria.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for NGU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 500/- for General/Unreserved candidates and Rs. 250/- for ST/SC/OBC/EWS/PwD candidates. Fee is payable through online mode only and is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q6. Is knowledge of Gujarati mandatory for NGU Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, knowledge of Gujarati is mandatory for all applicants regardless of department, as explicitly stated in the notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for NGU Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves three stages: (1) Academic Score-based shortlisting (as per UGC Regulation 2018, Appendix II Table 3A) in a 1:15 ratio, (2) Screening Test wherever necessary (syllabus published on the University website), and (3) Personal Interview for shortlisted candidates. Final selection is based solely on interview performance.

faq.question: Q8. How to apply for NGU Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must: (a) Apply online at https://recruitment.ngu.ac.in by 15-06-2026; (b) Pay the application fee online; and (c) Submit 3 sets of printed hardcopy application along with all self-attested documents and Academic Score Appendix II Table 3A to the Registrar, NGU, Patan – 384265 by 20-06-2026, 06:00 PM via Speed Post or in person.

faq.question: Q9. Which departments have vacancies in NGU Advertisement 05/2026?
faq.answer: The 17 posts are distributed across: Institute of Architecture (1), Department of Chemistry (2), Department of Mathematics (1), S.K. School of Business Management/MBA (3), Department of English (1), Department of Life Science — Microbiology & Zoology (2), Department of Hospital Management (3), and Department of Gujarati (4).

faq.question: Q10. Are there reservations for PwD candidates in NGU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: 2 posts are reserved for PwD candidates — 1 in Department of Life Science (Zoology) for Blind/Low Vision candidates (Second Attempt) and 1 in Department of Gujarati for Deaf/Hard of Hearing candidates. Only candidates with a benchmark disability of 40% or above, certified by a competent authority (Govt. Hospital Superintendent/Civil Surgeon/Medical Board), are eligible for PwD reservation benefits.
